title: U.S. Congressman Nick Begich Wants America to Stop Selling Its Bitcoin — And Start Treating It Like Gold
summary: U.S. Congressman Nick Begich argues the government should stop selling seized Bitcoin and instead hold it as a strategic reserve asset, similar to gold. He also calls for a cautious approach to AI, comparing it to nuclear technology. The proposal highlights a growing recognition of Bitcoin's long-term value.
body:
U.S. Congressman Nick Begich, a first-term representative from Alaska and former tech entrepreneur, has proposed that the United States should stop selling seized Bitcoin and instead treat it as a strategic reserve asset, much like gold. In an opinion piece published in Bitcoin Magazine, Begich argues that Bitcoin should be recognized for its long-term value and potential to strengthen the country's economic position.

Begich, who has a background in technology and entrepreneurship, also calls for a sober reckoning of artificial intelligence (AI), suggesting that the U.S. should approach AI with the same level of caution and strategic planning it applies to nuclear technology. He believes that Bitcoin and AI are both transformative technologies that require thoughtful policy and long-term planning.

The proposal underscores a shift in perspective among some policymakers who are beginning to see Bitcoin not just as a speculative asset but as a valuable reserve that could benefit the nation's economic stability. For everyday people, this could mean increased legitimacy and potential integration of Bitcoin into mainstream financial systems, offering more security and options for investment.

As the debate around Bitcoin's role in national economies continues, it will be important to watch how other governments respond to similar proposals. The outcome could influence global policies on cryptocurrency and shape the future of digital assets. For now, the conversation is just beginning, and the next steps will depend on further discussions and potential legislative actions.

Begich, a first-term Alaska rep and former tech entrepreneur, also calls for a cautious approach to AI, comparing it to nuclear technology. He sees Bitcoin as a long-term value play.

This proposal highlights a growing recognition of Bitcoin's potential. What happens next will depend on policy debates. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-17-congressman-proposes-us-treat-bitcoin-like-strategic-reserve-asset
